subject: feat(theme): dark-first default on first visit — flip from light-first
body:
First-visit users with no stored preference now land on dark mode
regardless of OS prefers-color-scheme. Mirror image of the previous
light-first policy:

- index.html bootstrap: storedDark === 'true' → storedDark !== 'false'.
  Absent storage → dark. Only the literal string 'false' opts into light.
- src/utils/theme.ts resolveInitial / setCombo / toggleDark / cleared-
  storage branch: coalesce null → true.
- meta name="theme-color" content + PWA manifest theme_color +
  background_color: #0082ce / #ffffff → #202020 / #202020 (business
  base-100). Splash → app chrome stays consistent for the majority
  who never toggle.
- Inline boot-chrome fallback hex (boot-loader, noscript, error overlay
  in index.html; failure overlay in main.tsx) flipped to the dark
  palette so the fallback path remains coherent if the bootstrap script
  is blocked (CSP, JS disabled).
- src/index.css plugin comment + CLAUDE.md THEME_DARK_MODE section
  rewritten to document the dark-first policy and the recovery paths
  (light-first revert + OS-sync restore).

Existing users: anyone who explicitly toggled (storage has darkMode=
true|false) keeps their preference. Anyone who never toggled (no
storage key) flips to dark on next visit.

Tags: theme, ui, user-facing

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
